steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: When you see your friend in the morning, what do you say?
questions.hint: You say this when you meet someone!

questions.question_text: Your teacher walks into the classroom. What can you say?
questions.hint: We say this at the start of the day.

questions.question_text: A new student comes to your class. How do you greet them?

items.definition: A greeting word
A casual greeting
To reply to a question
To hear attentively
A greeting you say when you meet someone
A friendly way to say hello
What you say when someone asks you a question
Use your ears to hear carefully

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👋

steps.title: First Day Greeting
steps.text: He says, 'Hello!' She says, 'Hi!' They listen and answer the teacher.

steps.explanation: We say 'Hello, I am' and then our name when we meet someone new.

items.explanation: Use 'is' with he, she, or it
Add 's' to verbs with he, she, or it
This is correct! We use 'is' with she.

items.question: Who ___ your teacher?
items.explanation: Use 'is' for asking about one person

items.question: Who ___ that boy?
items.explanation: Use 'is' with he, she, or it

items.question: Who ___ your friend?
items.explanation: Use 'is' when asking about one person

steps.audio_text: Good morning, children! Today we meet a new friend. Her name is Lucy. She says, 'Hello! Nice to meet you!' Listen carefully. Now, can you answer my question? Who is the new friend?

questions.question: What should you do when the teacher talks?

steps.prompt: Practice greeting someone new. Say these sentences out loud!

prompts.prompt: Say: Hello! My name is [your name].
Say: Hi! Nice to meet you!
Ask your friend: Who is your teacher?

questions.question_text: When you meet someone new, you say ___
Who ___ she?
What does 'listen' mean?
True or False: 'Hi' and 'Hello' are both greetings.
Your teacher asks a question. What do you do?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Who are our new friends today?
questions.hint: Look at the two new students in the picture!

questions.question_text: What do we say when we meet a new friend?
questions.hint: We use this word when we first see someone.

questions.question_text: How do Ben and Kim feel today?
questions.hint: Look at their faces. Are they smiling?

items.definition: Used to refer to two or more people
What someone is called
Not old or not known before
Having existed for a long time

items.hint: Not new, had it a long time

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👥

steps.title: Ben and Kim
steps.text: Today, Ben and Kim join our class. They are new. Ben is happy. Kim is shy.

steps.explanation: Use 'He' for a boy, 'She' for a girl, and 'They' for two or more friends.

items.sentence: They are called Ben and Kim.
items.corrected: They are called Ben and Kim.

items.question: ___ are my friends Ben and Kim.
items.explanation: Use 'They' for two or more people

steps.audio_text: Hello class! Today we have two new friends. They are called Ben and Kim. Ben is seven years old. Kim is six years old. Let's say hello to our new friends!

steps.prompt: Practice introducing yourself and your friends!

prompts.prompt: Say: Hello, my name is [Your Name].
Say: He is my new friend.

questions.question_text: What does 'new' mean?
He ___ my new friend.
Which word means 'more than one person'?
Read: 'She is called Kim. She is new.' Is Kim a new student?
Choose the correct sentence:

steps.instruction: Let's warm up with some alphabet fun!

questions.question_text: What letter does your name start with?
questions.correct_answer: The first letter I hear in my name
options: The first letter I hear in my name
The last letter I hear in my name
questions.hint: Say your name slowly. What sound do you hear first?

questions.question_text: Can you sing the ABC song?
options: No, I don't know it yet
I can sing some of it
I need help to sing it
questions.hint: A-B-C-D-E-F-G... Do you know this song?

questions.question_text: How many letters are in the alphabet?
questions.hint: There are quite a lot! More than 20 but less than 30.

items.definition: A set of letters
Say letters in order
A symbol in words
A group of letters

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 🔤

steps.title: The Spelling Game
steps.text: In class, they play a spelling game. She spells 'dog'. He spells 'cat'. They clap for the winner!

steps.rule_pattern: The letter ___ is in the word ___.
steps.explanation: We use letters to make words!
examples: The letter A is in the word apple.
The letter B is in the word ball.
The letter C is in the word cat.

items.sentence: The letter are on the paper.

items.explanation: Correct! This is a proper question

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Today we're learning the alphabet. Emma can spell her name: E-M-M-A. Tom can spell his name too: T-O-M. They both know all their letters!

questions.question: How many letters are in Emma's name?
What are the children learning today?

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking! Say these sentences clearly.

prompts.prompt: Say: She is learning the alphabet.
Say: He is spelling a word.

questions.question_text: Which word means the letters A to Z?
She ___ learning her letters.
What does 'spell' mean?
questions.correct_answer: Say the letters in a word
options: Say the letters in a word

questions.question_text: True or False: 'They are writing words' is correct grammar.
How many letters are in the word 'cat'?

steps.instruction: Let's review! Answer these questions about saying hello!

questions.question_text: When you meet your teacher in the morning, what do you say?
questions.hint: We say this friendly word when we see someone for the first time that day.

questions.question_text: What do you say when someone asks 'What's your name?'
questions.hint: You tell them what people call you!

questions.question_text: When a friend says 'How are you?' what can you say?
questions.hint: You tell your friend how you feel today.

items.definition: A friendly word you say when you meet someone
What you say or do when someone asks you a question
When you use your ears to hear someone talking
What people call you
Something you just got or just started
When you say the letters in a word one by one
A, B, C, D are all examples of this
Letters put together to make something you can say
A short, friendly greeting
A word for two or more people or things
Something from a long time ago, not new
All the letters from A to Z together
What you do with your ears to hear
What your friends call you
Say all the letters in order: H-E-L-L-O
Cat, dog, and run are all examples of this

items.question: When you meet your teacher, what do you say?
What do you do when the teacher asks you a question?
What do you use to hear your teacher talking?
This means more than one person. 'Tom and Sue are friends. _____ play together.'
A, B, C, D, E, F... What is this called?
Your toy is not old. It is _____.
When you say C-A-T, you _____ the word.
What do you call this? 📛 'My _____ is Emma.'

steps.title: New Friends
steps.text: They are new. Hello! My name is Tim. Who is she? She is Ana. Let's listen and answer.

steps.rule_pattern: He is ___ / She is ___ / They are ___
steps.explanation: We use 'he' for boys, 'she' for girls, and 'they' for two or more people.

items.explanation: Use 'He' at the start of a sentence, not 'Him'
Say 'a book' when you have one
Use 'is' with 'alphabet' because it is one thing
Say 'What is your name?' not 'Who are'

items.question: ___ is my new friend.
items.explanation: Use 'He' at the start of a sentence

items.question: ___ are new students.
items.explanation: Use 'They' for more than one person

steps.audio_text: Good morning, class! Today we have two new students. Her name is Emma and his name is Tom. They will learn the alphabet with us. Emma, can you spell your name? Listen carefully: E-M-M-A. Great! Now Tom will spell his name too.

questions.question: How many new students are there?
What will the new students learn?

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking! Say these sentences about you and your friends.

prompts.prompt: Say hello and tell me your name.
Can you spell your name? Say the letters.
prompts.expected_text: My name is [name]. I can spell it: [letters].

prompts.prompt: Tell me about your friend. Use 'he' or 'she'.
prompts.expected_text: He is my friend. His name is [name]. OR She is my friend. Her name is [name].

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet someone?
She ___ my new teacher.
When you say the letters in a word one by one, you ___ it.
___ are new students in our class.
Read: 'Tom and Emma are friends. They listen to the alphabet.' Who listens to the alphabet?
